title: Supreme Court Sides with Hikma in Landmark Skinny-Label Patent Case (Vascepa)
headline: The U.S.
key_facts: SCOTUS sided with Hikma; ruling concerns "skinny label" generics (generics that carve out patented indications) Affects how generics can be marketed when some uses remain under patent Published June 4, 2026

title: ADC Therapeutics Crashes >50% on Zynlonta Death Rate in Phase 3 Trial
headline: Shares of ADC Therapeutics plunged more than 50% after a Phase 3 trial of its blood cancer drug Zynlonta (loncastuximab tesirine) showed a death rate that analysts say "may be difficult to accept for physicians, patients and regulators."
key_facts: Stock fell >50% (BioPharma Dive) Phase 3 trial observed elevated death rate (specific numbers behind paywall) At least one analyst stated the safety signal may be insurmountable for regulatory/clinical acceptance

title: Legend Biotech Surges on Early "In Vivo" Lymphoma Cell Therapy Data
headline: Legend Biotech shares surged after reporting early data showing its "in vivo" CAR-T cell therapy reduced or eliminated disease signs in all recipients in lymphoma.
key_facts: All recipients showed reduced or eliminated disease signs "In vivo" approach — therapy manufactured inside the body, not extracted ("ex vivo") Could compete with established ex vivo therapies like Novartis' Kymriah

title: Biotech M&A Hits $106 Billion in 2026, On Track for Best Year Since Pre-Covid
headline: Global biopharma M&A has reached $106 billion across 201 deals in 2026, on pace to exceed $250 billion for the full year — the strongest since 2019.
key_facts: $106 billion across 201 transactions YTD (PitchBook data) Average deal value: $527.3 million (up from $365 million in 2025) 2025 total was $209 billion; 2024 was $114.8 billion

title: House Bill (COINS Act) Aims to Crack Down on China Biotech Deals
headline: A proposed House bill would add biotechnology to the COINS Act, restricting U.S.
key_facts: Follows two major deals involving Pfizer and Bristol Myers Squibb with Chinese companies Comes amid broader U.S. fears about China's drugmaking advancement Introduced alongside ASCO 2026 where Chinese biotech presence was notably expanded

title: Acting FDA Chief Not Interested in Permanent Role; Pledges Against Political Int
headline: The acting FDA commissioner stated publicly that they are not interested in the permanent commissioner role, while also pledging to prevent political interference in rare disease decisions.
key_facts: Published ~3 hours before Endpoints snapshot (approximately midday June 4) Separate statement on preventing political interference in rare disease meeting

title: Novo Nordisk Indiana Manufacturing Site Under FDA Scrutiny Again
headline: Novo Nordisk's manufacturing facility in Indiana is under renewed FDA scrutiny, with reports citing mammalian hair contamination and roof leaks.
key_facts: Issues include mammalian hair found on-site and roof leaks Described as repeat scrutiny ("once again") Endpoints initially published then issued a correction

title: Otsuka Seeks Full Approval for Voxzact (IgA Nephropathy)
headline: Otsuka is presenting new evidence supporting conversion from accelerated to full approval for Voxzact in IgA nephropathy.
key_facts: Drug name: Voxzact (note: Endpoints spells it "Voyx​act" — possible variant/typo) Seeking full FDA approval (currently under accelerated approval)

title: Fulcrum Therapeutics Exploring Sale After FDA Sidelined Sickle Cell Drug
headline: Fulcrum Therapeutics is seeking an exit/sale after regulatory feedback ended its sickle cell disease ambitions.
key_facts: Fulcrum Therapeutics is seeking an exit/sale after regulatory feedback ended its sickle cell disease ambitions.
